sector.title: Investment Sector
sector.description: An investment sector refers to a specific area of the economy. This could be anything from technology and healthcare to real estate and renewable energy. By keeping track of your investments, you can diversify your portfolio and find investment that align with your interests, risk tolerance, and investment goals.

descriptions.Bonds: Debt securities issued by governments or corporations. Bondholders lend money to the issuer in exchange for periodic interest payments and the repayment of the principal amount at maturity.
descriptions.Convertible Note: A convertible note is a flexible debt investment that can be converted into equity at a future date. Investors earn interest and have the option to convert their debt into equity at a predetermined valuation.
descriptions.Loan: Provides regular interest payments and repayment of the loan at maturity. Generally considered to be less risky than shares, but also provides lower returns.
descriptions.Publicly Traded Shares: Shares of a company that are traded on a public stock exchange. These shares can be bought and sold by anyone, and their prices fluctuate based on market demand and company performance.
descriptions.Private Shares: Shares of a company that are not publicly traded. These shares are typically held by a limited number of investors, such as founders, employees, and private equity firms. Private shares can be more illiquid, meaning they may be difficult to sell, but they can also offer higher potential returns than public shares.
descriptions.SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future): A SAFE is a simplified investment contract commonly used by early-stage startups, offering a quicker and less complex alternative to traditional term sheets. Investors who purchase a SAFE are essentially buying a future equity stake in the company, often with a discount on the future valuation.
descriptions.Funds: A pool of money from multiple investors, managed by a professional fund manager. Funds invest in a variety of assets, including stocks, bonds, and real estate, to diversify risk and generate returns.
descriptions.Commodities: Physical assets, such as gold, oil, and agricultural products, that are traded on commodity exchanges. Investing in commodities can be a way to hedge against inflation and economic uncertainty.
descriptions.Futures: Contracts that obligate the buyer to purchase or the seller to sell a specific asset at a future date at a predetermined price. Futures contracts are often used by traders to speculate on price movements and to hedge against risk.
descriptions.Crypto: Digital or virtual currencies that use cryptography for security. Cryptocurrencies can be highly volatile and speculative, but they also offer the potential for significant returns.

descriptions.Pre-Seed: At this very early stage, the company often has an innovative idea but no product or service on the market yet. The risk is high, but the potential for high returns is also great.
descriptions.Seed: The company has a working prototype or MVP (Minimum Viable Product) and is seeking funding to scale the business. The risk is still high, but less than in the pre-seed phase.
descriptions.Series A: The company has a product or service on the market and is starting to generate revenue. Investments at this stage are often used to expand the business and increase market share.
descriptions.Series B: The company is well-established and has a strong market position. Investments at this stage are often used to fund product development, marketing, and expansion into new markets.
descriptions.Initial Public Offering: Initial Public Offering, i.e. the initial listing on the stock exchange.

minimal-ticket-size.title: Minimal Ticket Size
minimal-ticket-size.description: Ticket size is a term commonly used in investing and simply refers to the size of an investment. It is the total amount an investor puts into a specific project or company.

target.title: Target
target.description: The Investment Target for this deal represents the total amount of capital needed to fund this project or the current investment round.

return-rate.title: Internal Rate of Return (IRR)
return-rate.description: The Internal Rate of Return (IRR) is a financial metric used to estimate the profitability of potential investments. It represents the expected annualized rate of return that an investment is anticipated to generate. A higher IRR generally indicates a more attractive investment opportunity.

risk-factor.title: Risk Factor
risk-factor.description:
Risk factors are conditions, situations, or events that increases the likelihood of an unfavorable outcome, such as a loss on an investment. These can be both internal factors, related to the specific investment or company, and external factors, influenced by market conditions or economic trends.

We identify and assess risk factors and summarize them into three risk levels; low, medium and high.

SecondaryPage.title: Secondary
SecondaryPage.description:
Secondaries offer a unique opportunity to invest in established, high-growth companies. By purchasing shares directly from existing investors, you can gain exposure to promising businesses that aren't publicly traded. This strategy can provide a more diversified portfolio and potentially higher returns.

We are developing our platform so you will be able to read about, buy, and sell unlisted shares directly on the platform. Until that is ready, we operate as a traditional broker via email and phone. Register your interest here, and we will get back to you!
SecondaryPage.question: Are you interested to buy and/or sell secondary?
SecondaryPage.interest-confirmation: We have received your expression of interest and will be in touch via email or phone using the contact information in your profile.
SecondaryPage.no-options-selected: You have to choose at least one option to express interest.

Errors.incorrect-email-format: Enter an email in a valid format e.g. name@email.com
Errors.incorrect-phone-format: Enter a phone number in a valid format e.g. +46723456789
Errors.auth/invalid-credential: Wrong password. Try again or click Forgot Password to reset it.
Errors.auth/weak-password: Password is too weak! At least 8 characters.
Errors.auth/password-does-not-meet-requirements:
You need a stronger password.

The password needs to meet these requirements:
- At least one uppercase letter (A-Z)
- At least one lowercase letter (a-z)
- At least one numeric digit (0-9)
- At least one special character (e.g., !@#$%^&*)
- A minimum length of 8 characters
Errors.auth/account-exists-with-different-crede: It looks like you've already used this email address to sign up another way. Please try logging in using the same way as you did during sign up.
Errors.auth/operation-not-allowed: Phone verification is enabled, but Firebase blocked this SMS request. Please check the Firebase SMS region policy and billing settings.
Errors.auth/sms-region-not-allowed: SMS verification is blocked for this phone region. Enable the destination country in Firebase Authentication → Settings → SMS region policy, then try again.
Errors.auth/invalid-verification-code: Wrong verification code. Please try again.
Errors.auth/invalid-phone-number: Invalid phone number. Please check the country code and number.
Errors.auth/quota-exceeded: Too many attempts. Please try again later.
Errors.auth/captcha-check-failed: reCAPTCHA verification failed. Please refresh and try again.
Errors.auth/app-verification-failed: App verification failed. Please refresh the page and try again.
Errors.auth/unauthorized-domain: This domain is not authorized for Firebase phone verification. Add app.kapita.com to Firebase Authentication → Settings → Authorized domains.
Errors.auth/no-current-user: Please log in with your email again before verifying your phone.
Errors.auth/network-request-failed: Network error while sending the SMS. Please check your connection and try again.
Errors.auth/signicat-login-failed: Could not complete Signicat login. Please try again.
Errors.auth/provider-already-linked: This phone number is already verified for your account.
Errors.auth/credential-already-in-use: This phone number is already linked to another account.
Errors.auth/too-many-requests: Too many attempts. Please try again later.
Errors.auth/billing-not-enabled: SMS verification requires Firebase billing to be enabled. Please contact support.
Errors.auth/unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.
Errors.auth/signicat-login-failed: Login with Signicat failed. Please try again.
Errors.auth/signicat-config: Signicat configuration error. Please contact support.
Errors.auth/signicat-state: Invalid login state. Please try again.
Errors.auth/signicat-user: Unable to retrieve user information. Please try again.
Errors.auth/signicat-callback: Login callback failed. Please try again.
